channel.title: My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Compiler
item.description: Compilers Scanning A scanner converts source code into tokens. Deterministic Finite Automata (DFA) A deterministic finite automata is a tuple $(S, \Sigma, \delta, s_0, S_A)$ where: $S$ is a finite set of states $\Sigma$ is a finite set of alphabets $\delta(s, c)$ is a transition function that maps each state $s \in S$ and character $c \in \Sigma$ to the next state $s_0 \in S$ is the starting state $S_A$ is the set of accepting states A DFA can be used to recognize certain patterns of words.
